title: Seed GitHub Pages with tool inventory
description:
Create an initial GitHub Pages site for this repository that publishes a concise inventory of the tools and first-party surfaces available to agents/operators.

Scope:
- Add/enable a minimal GitHub Pages-compatible docs site for the repo.
- Include a tool inventory covering the Cacophony surfaces used by this project, such as bd, agent, msg, scratch, test, build, choices, cron, image generation, Pi loop/self-nudge/self-ops, tmux-cli, Tendril, and relevant repo-local tools.
- Keep the inventory generated or easy to update where practical; avoid duplicating stale long-form docs unnecessarily.
- Document how to preview/build the Pages content locally.
- Ensure the site can be published by GitHub Pages without requiring secrets or local-only state.

Acceptance criteria:
- A docs or Pages entry point exists and renders a tool inventory.
- The repo documents the Pages publishing path and local preview/build command.
- Validation appropriate for the chosen static site setup passes.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

dispatch_payload.goal:
Implement bead bd-a253c3: Seed GitHub Pages with tool inventory

## Description
Create an initial GitHub Pages site for this repository that publishes a concise inventory of the tools and first-party surfaces available to agents/operators.

Scope:
- Add/enable a minimal GitHub Pages-compatible docs site for the repo.
- Include a tool inventory covering the Cacophony surfaces used by this project, such as bd, agent, msg, scratch, test, build, choices, cron, image generation, Pi loop/self-nudge/self-ops, tmux-cli, Tendril, and relevant repo-local tools.
- Keep the inventory generated or easy to update where practical; avoid duplicating stale long-form docs unnecessarily.
- Document how to preview/build the Pages content locally.
- Ensure the site can be published by GitHub Pages without requiring secrets or local-only state.

Acceptance criteria:
- A docs or Pages entry point exists and renders a tool inventory.
- The repo documents the Pages publishing path and local preview/build command.
- Validation appropriate for the chosen static site setup passes.

## Ownership
- This bead has already been claimed by the spawn-and-claim surface.
- Do NOT re-claim it or call `caco bd claim`.
- You own reintegration: finish via `caco agent complete`.
- Do NOT manually close the bead with `caco bd close`.

## Instructions
1. Start with `caco bd show --bead-id bd-a253c3` to hydrate the full context
2. Implement the changes described above
3. Run tests and clippy to verify
4. Commit your changes with the bead id in the message
5. Complete the agent via: caco agent complete --id $CACOPHONY_AGENT --mode direct

## Handling Unrelated Test Failures
If tests fail for reasons unrelated to your changes:
1. Coordinate ownership in project chat first: announce the failing test with `caco msg broadcast --project $CACOPHONY_PROJECT --body "[broken-on-main] taking <test_name>"` and check `caco msg inbox --project $CACOPHONY_PROJECT` so you do not duplicate work
2. Check `caco bd search --test-name "<test_name>"` for existing breakage beads across all statuses before filing a new broken-on-main bead
3. If none found, file: `caco bd create --title "[broken-on-main] <test_name> failing" --labels test-failure,broken-on-main --priority 1` with error details
4. If another agent already owns the failure, avoid duplicate changes and coordinate a handoff; otherwise take ownership and fix the failing test before completion
5. Do not skip or #[ignore] the failing test by default unless a controller, operator, or confirmed owner has explicitly taken responsibility
